mission.title: external-secrets: Support google workload identity federation
mission.description: Support google workload identity federation. Users encounter: "will occur.".

steps.title: Identify external-secrets feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your external-secrets deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=external-secrets
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `will occur.`

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant external-secrets resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
Support for workload identity federation using credential configuration files.

Currently, if the credential configuration files is specified as secretAccessKeySecretRef, the following error will occu

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support google workload identity federation
steps.description:
## Problem Statement

The GCPSM documentation was missing the description of the (arguably) simplest WIF approach, and in my experience the order made it difficult for newcomers to understand the available authentication options and implications.

## Related Issue

N/A

## Proposed Changes

I revise
```yaml
failed to create GCP secretmanager client: failed to process the provided JSON credentials: google: read JWT from JSON credentials: 'type' field is "external_account" (expected "service_account")
```

steps.title: Confirm Support google workload identity federation is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `will occur.` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: ## Problem Statement

The GCPSM documentation was missing the description of the (arguably) simplest WIF approach, and in my experience the order made it difficult for newcomers to understand the available authentication options and implications.

## Related Issue

N/A

## Proposed Changes

I revised the authentication section and the examples, demonstrating the essential steps/mechanisms of each . This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets: failed to create GCP secretmanager client: failed to process the provided JSON credentials: google: read JWT from JSON credentials: 'type' field is "external_account" (expected "service_account")

apiVersion: generators.external-secrets.io/v1alpha1
kind: GCRAccessToken
metadata:
  name: gcr-gen
spec:
  projectID: "my-project"
  auth:
    jwt:
        workloadIdentityPool: foo
        provider: bar
        serviceAccountRef:
	    name: service-account-token-to-use
            audience:
	        - foo
	        - bar

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with external-secrets installed or the issue environment reproducible.
